title: ATAC-STARR-seq
author: Tyler Hansen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2nuqdew
materials: Anneal Adaptor solutions separately with thermocycler conditions as follows: 95°C, 3 min 65°C, 3 min Ramp to 24°C, -1ºC per cycle, 30 s Hold at 24°C forever
Turn on all equipment! Set the microcentrifuge to 4ºC & the thermomixer to 37ºC. Each of these takes 15-30min to get to their desired temp.
Let the beads dry but not over dry.

*Note: at this step, you will generate the vector required for the gibson cloning reaction. This linearized vector contains the homology arms for gibson. In the NAR paper we used an oligo to add the N504 barcode to the plasmid, this is not required for most purposes. For this reason, I’ve denoted the reverse primer as N5XX/universal because you can add whatever index you want OR not add an index at all, just like the forward primer. PCR amplify vector backbone from the hSTARR-Seq_ORI plasmid (Addgene #99296) with the mixture and thermocycler conditions below. 98ºC, 30 sec 2 cycles of 98ºC, 10 sec 59ºC, 30 sec 72ºC, 90 sec 28 cycles of 98ºC, 10 sec 72ºC, 2min Final extension 72ºC, 2 min
After recovery make a dilution series:
Aliquot 50 μL mix into PCR tubes.
Aliquot five volumes of 1mL into Invitrogen™ Phasemaker™ Tubes.
Isolate Poly(A)+ RNA from Total RNA:
Make MasterMix following the recipe below:

steps.title: Dilute oligos to 100 µM in STE buffer
steps.instruction: Dilute oligos to 100 µM in STE buffer.

steps.title: Make Adaptor Mixes in 200μL PCR tubes
steps.instruction: Make Adaptor Mixes in 200μL PCR tubes: Mix (Adaptor A) [50µL]: 5µL Tn5MERV oligo (100µM) 5µL Tn5_1 Oligo (100µM) 40µL H2O Mix (Adaptor B) [50µL]: 5µL Tn5MERV oligo (100µM) 5µL Tn5_2_ME_Comp Oligo (100µM) 40µL H2O

steps.title: After annealing mix
steps.instruction: After annealing mix: 50µL Adaptor solution A 50µL Adaptor solution B 100µL Glycerol (≥99.5%) 200 µL Adaptor mixture, which is now 5 µM.

steps.title: Mix the following:10 µL 5µM Adaptors...
steps.instruction: Mix the following:10 µL 5µM Adaptors Mix10 µL Tn5 (3mg/mL in Tn5 storage buffer)20 µL Total Reaction Volume

steps.title: Let stand at room temp for...
steps.instruction: Let stand at room temp for 30-60min, then immediately place on ice. Assembled transposome may be stored at -20°C for several months.

steps.title: Count cells in duplicate using a hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Count cells in duplicate using a hemocytometer. Record cell count. Note: It is important that cell viability is greater than 85%. If less than 85%, I strongly recommend trying again when viability is much better.

steps.title: In two tubes, pellet 200,000 viable...
steps.instruction: In two tubes, pellet 200,000 viable cells in each tube at 500 RCF at 4°C for 10 min in a fixed angle centrifuge. Use low-bind Eppendorf tubes to prevent pellets from sticking along the back of the tube (this is much more important for reproducibility than you might think).

steps.title: ATAC-Resuspension Buffer (RSB) containing 0
steps.instruction: ATAC-Resuspension Buffer (RSB) containing 0.1% NP40, 0.1% Tween-20, and 0.01% digitonin (lysis buffer): For 110μL, mix 1.1μL 10% Tween-20,1.1μL 10% )" target="_blank" rel="noopener">IGEPAL CA-630 , 1.1μL 1% digitonin, 106.7μL 1x RSB.

steps.title: ATAC-RSB containing only 0.1% Tween-20 (wash out buffer)
steps.instruction: ATAC-RSB containing only 0.1% Tween-20 (wash out buffer): For 2.2mL, mix 220μL 10% Tween-20 and 2mL 1x RSB. Keep on ice.

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant and resuspend each cell...
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant and resuspend each cell pellet in 50µL cold ATAC-Resuspension Buffer (RSB) containing 0.1% IGEPAL CA-630, 0.1% Tween-20, and 0.01% Digitonin and pipette up and down 5 times. Note: The volume used here is a modification to the Omin-ATAC protocol. In my hands, 50μL for this number of cells works just as well as 200μL and is much more manageable.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Wash out lysis with 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Wash out lysis with 1 ml of cold ATAC-RSB containing ONLY 0.1% Tween-20 and invert tube 3 times to mix. Note: The volume used here is a modification to the Omin-ATAC protocol. In my hands, 1mL for this number of cells works just as well as 4mL and is much more manageable.

steps.title: Immediately pellet nuclei at 500 RCF...
steps.instruction: Immediately pellet nuclei at 500 RCF for 10min at 4°C in a fixed angle centrifuge. While waiting, make transposition mix:

steps.title: Aspirate all supernatant, carefully avoiding visible...
steps.instruction: Aspirate all supernatant, carefully avoiding visible cell pellet, using two pipetting steps (aspirate down to 100 ul with a p1000 pipette and remove final 100 ul with a p200 pipette).

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellets in 180µL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellets in 180µL of transposition mix by pipetting up and down 10 times. Combine like samples for a total volume of 360μL.

steps.title: Add 40µL assembled Tn5 transposase and...
steps.instruction: Add 40µL assembled Tn5 transposase and quickly vortex to mix well. This is the equivalent to 5μL/reaction.

steps.title: Incubate reaction at 37°C for 30min...
steps.instruction: Incubate reaction at 37°C for 30min in a thermomixer with 1000 RPM mixing.

steps.title: After incubation, pool the eight like-reactions...
steps.instruction: After incubation, pool the eight like-reactions together in a 15mL conical tube containing 2mL DNA binding Buffer.

steps.title: Purify the small-isolated DNA fragments using the Zymo C+C kit
steps.instruction: Purify the small-isolated DNA fragments using the Zymo C+C kit. Run all 2.4mL through one column, adding in 800μL increments. Elute in 22µl pre-warmed elution buffer. Incubate on column for 5min. Can stop here and store at -20°C.

steps.title: Make PCR mastermix
steps.instruction: Make PCR mastermix: Note: do not use a hot-start polymerase here. The reaction requires polymerase activity in the initial extension step.

steps.title: Run PCR with the following thermocycler parameters
steps.instruction: Run PCR with the following thermocycler parameters: 72 ºC, 5 min 98ºC, 30 sec 4 cycles of 98ºC, 10 sec 62ºC, 30 sec 72ºC, 1 min Final extension 72ºC, 2 min

steps.title: Purify PCR Product using Zymo Clean and Concentrator Kit
steps.instruction: Purify PCR Product using Zymo Clean and Concentrator Kit. Mix 250μL Binding Buffer with 50μL PCR products. Elute in 32µl pre-warmed elution buffer. Incubate on column for 5min.

steps.title: Analyze by running 2uL on High-Sensitivity D5000 Tapestation
steps.instruction: Analyze by running 2uL on High-Sensitivity D5000 Tapestation. Determine size distribution and molarity. If it looks good, move on to cloning. Need between 0.04pmols and 0.27pmols total—no more, no less. If there are too many large fragments, perform the SPRI bead size selection below.

steps.title: *Note
steps.instruction: *Note: Perform a 0.6X ratio right-side selection to remove fragments larger than 500bp. The volumes listed below are for that ratio specifically. Reference the SPRISelect User Manual if this needs to be altered. Thoroughly shake the SPRIselect bottle to resuspend the SPRI beads.

steps.title: Add 18μL of SPRIselect to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 18μL of SPRIselect to the sample. Pipette up and down and vortex for at least 5 seconds to mix thoroughly. Note: Insufficient mixing of sample and SPRIselect will lead to inconsistent size selection results.

steps.title: Place the reaction vessel on DynaMag-PCR...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction vessel on DynaMag-PCR magnet and allow the SPRI beads to settle to the magnet for ~1min.

steps.title: Transfer the clear supernatant, which contains...
steps.instruction: Transfer the clear supernatant, which contains the Right-Side Size Selected sample, to a new reaction vessel. The reaction vessel with the remaining beads can be discarded.

steps.title: Add 36μL of SPRIselect to the Right-Side Size Selected sample
steps.instruction: Add 36μL of SPRIselect to the Right-Side Size Selected sample. Pipette up and down and vortex for at least 5 seconds to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Place the reaction vessel on an...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction vessel on an appropriate magnetic stand or plate and allow the SPRI beads to settle to the magnet for ~1min.

steps.title: Remove and discard the clear supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the clear supernatant.

steps.title: With the reaction vessel still on...
steps.instruction: With the reaction vessel still on the magnet, add 180 μL of freshly-made 85% ethanol and incubate at RT for 30 seconds. Remove and discard ALL of the ethanol supernatant.

steps.title: Once cracks are starting to form...
steps.instruction: Once cracks are starting to form in the pellet, elute by removing the reaction vessel from the magnet and adding 12μL of 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, elution buffer, or nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Pipette up and down and vortex...
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down and vortex for at least 5 seconds to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Place the reaction vessel on an...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction vessel on an appropriate magnetic stand or plate and allow the SPRI beads to settle to the magnet for ~1min.

steps.title: Transfer the eluate (size selected sample)...
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluate (size selected sample) to an appropriate storage vessel. Note: It is very important to avoid pipetting beads over to the new tube. They can cause the tape station to run inaccurately.

steps.title: Analyze size-selected sample by running on...
steps.instruction: Analyze size-selected sample by running on 2uL on High-Sensitivity D5000 Tapestation and analyze for concentration and size range.

steps.title: Purify PCR Product using Zymo Clean...
steps.instruction: Purify PCR Product using Zymo Clean and Concentrator – 5 Kit. Elute in 20μL elution buffer.

steps.title: Analyze by running 0
steps.instruction: Analyze by running 0.1ng on 1% agarose gel with EtBr stain to determine purity.

steps.title: *This part of the protocol is...
steps.instruction: *This part of the protocol is intentionally flexible in order to add the entire volume of tagments to these gibson reactions. In this step, perform four Gibson reactions per sample at a 1:2 rato of vector:insert. Note: this used to be a 1:3 ratio but was changed to 1:2 after learning that 1:2 would require fewer PCR cycles and is the recommended ratio for this enzyme cocktail. The insert (tagments) should be at a total amount of 0.04pmols and 0.27pmols. Calculate the reaction molarity as follows:

steps.title: In 4 PCR tubes, combine the...
steps.instruction: In 4 PCR tubes, combine the volumes of insert and vector as written above.

steps.title: Bring up to a total volume of 5μL with water
steps.instruction: Bring up to a total volume of 5μL with water. Note: The total volume cannot exceed 5μL .

steps.title: In a separate PCR tube, preparea...
steps.instruction: In a separate PCR tube, preparea negative control where you substitute the inserts for water (only 1 reaction).

steps.title: Add 5μL of NEBuilder Hifi DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 5μL of NEBuilder Hifi DNA Assembly 2x MasterMix and mix well.

steps.title: Incubate at 50°C for 1hr
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50°C for 1hr.

steps.title: *This is needed to prevent arching in the Electroporation step
steps.instruction: *This is needed to prevent arching in the Electroporation step. DO NOT SKIP. Pool all four like-reactions together into a low-bind Eppendorf tube .

steps.title: Adjust volume to 222
steps.instruction: Adjust volume to 222.5μL by adding 182.5μL elution buffer (212.5μL to negative control).

steps.title: Add 25μL 3M NaAc pH5
steps.instruction: Add 25μL 3M NaAc pH5.2 and Vortex

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5μL GlycoBlue and Vortex.

steps.title: Add 750μL cold (-20°C) 100% EtOH and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 750μL cold (-20°C) 100% EtOH and vortex.

steps.title: Incubate at -20°C for 30-120min
steps.instruction: Incubate at -20°C for 30-120min.

steps.title: Spin at full speed for 30min @ 4°C
steps.instruction: Spin at full speed for 30min @ 4°C

steps.title: Wash with 750μL cold 70% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash with 750μL cold 70% EtOH. Spin at full speed for 15min @ 4°C.

steps.title: Dry pellet at room temp until dry
steps.instruction: Dry pellet at room temp until dry.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 10μL 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 10μL 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0. Pipette up/down until reconstituted. Can stop here, store @ -20°C.

steps.title: *In addition to the negative control,...
steps.instruction: *In addition to the negative control, do a positive control using the supplied pUC19. For the controls, only do one reaction and use refrozen cells if possible. Pre-cool pipette tips, GenePulser cuvettes, and 1.5mL tubes at -20°C. Prewarm 2L LB+amp flasks and SOC @ 37°C.

steps.title: Add all 10μL of each gibson...
steps.instruction: Add all 10μL of each gibson sample to a pre-cooled tube (2.5μL for controls). Keep on ice.

steps.title: Thaw MegaX DH10B cells on ice
steps.instruction: Thaw MegaX DH10B cells on ice. Each tube contains ~120μL .

steps.title: Add 80μL of thawed cells to...
steps.instruction: Add 80μL of thawed cells to each 10μL gibson sample and 20μL to each 2.5μL control using pre-cooled tips. Keep on ice.

steps.title: Pipette SLOWLY up/down to mix with pre-cooled tips
steps.instruction: Pipette SLOWLY up/down to mix with pre-cooled tips. Then add 20μL (max volume) to a GenePulser 0.1cm cuvette—may need to use 3-4 cuvettes for each sample, but only one for each control. Keep cuvettes on ice. Note: make sure the volume is touching both metal plates, otherwise it will arc (the best technique here is to pipette into the corner of the cuvette at an angle so the cells reach the bottom). To help, gently tap to the bottom.

steps.title: Electroporate with “exponential decay” pulse type...
steps.instruction: Electroporate with “exponential decay” pulse type at 2kV, 200Ω, and 25μF - (Gap distance is 0.1cm for brown-capped cuvettes).

steps.title: Immediately add 1mL pre-warmed SOC media...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 1mL pre-warmed SOC media to the 20μL DNA/bacteria mixture in the cuvette.

steps.title: Combine like-reactions into a round bottom 14mL culture tube
steps.instruction: Combine like-reactions into a round bottom 14mL culture tube.

steps.title: Recover at 37°C for 1 hour in shaker incubator
steps.instruction: Recover at 37°C for 1 hour in shaker incubator.

steps.title: Plate 100μL of each dilution onto a prewarmed LB+ampicillin(100μg/mL) plate
steps.instruction: Plate 100μL of each dilution onto a prewarmed LB+ampicillin(100μg/mL) plate. Incubate at 37°C for ~16hrs.

steps.title: With the remaining undiluted culture, add...
steps.instruction: With the remaining undiluted culture, add directly to 1L pre-warmed liquid LB+ ampicillin(100μg/mL) media. Shake at 37°C O/N 225 RPM. Do not do this for the controls! DO NOT FORGET TO ADD THE AMPICILLIN TO THE 1L CULTURE!!!

steps.title: Count colonies on plates after overnight...
steps.instruction: Count colonies on plates after overnight growth either manually or via automated means (imaging and particle counting with imageJ) The equations below assume a plating of 100µL from a 1mL dilution preparation: (.01 colony count) * 1000 = # colonies in 1mL of recovery media (.001 colony count) * 10000 = # colonies in 1mL of recovery media (.0001 colony count) * 100000 = # colonies in 1mL of recovery media

steps.title: Record OD 600
steps.instruction: Record OD 600 .

steps.title: Pellet 1L culture at 3400xg for 10min
steps.instruction: Pellet 1L culture at 3400xg for 10min.

steps.title: Proceed with Zymo Plasmid GigaPrep Kit...
steps.instruction: Proceed with Zymo Plasmid GigaPrep Kit protocol according to manufacturer directions.

steps.title: Elute with 2mL ZymoPURE Elution Buffer
steps.instruction: Elute with 2mL ZymoPURE Elution Buffer. Nanodrop for purity and concentration. Store DNA at -20°C.

steps.title: *Before proceeding to electroporation, it is...
steps.instruction: *Before proceeding to electroporation, it is important to determine whether the library has sufficient complexity. To do this, perform limited-cycle PCR to add illumina adapters and amplify the insert off the plasmid. Then submit the sample sequencing and target 15 million reads. Must be PE, but the cycle number is irrelevant as long as reads can be mapped. * To reduce rxn bias effect, perform two PCRs for each sample. Prepare a mastermix:

steps.title: Vortex and centrifuge, briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex and centrifuge, briefly. Run the following PCR reaction: 98ºC, 30 sec 8 cycles of 98ºC, 10 sec 68ºC, 30 sec 72ºC, 30 sec Final extension 72ºC, 2 min

steps.title: Pool like-reactions and then purify using...
steps.instruction: Pool like-reactions and then purify using Zymo Clean and Concentrate - 5 kit – elute in 30μL elution buffer (warm buffer to 55°C or 37°C prior to elution and incubate on column for 5min).

steps.title: Determine concentration and size distribution via...
steps.instruction: Determine concentration and size distribution via HS D5000 ScreenTape on the Agilent Tapestation. Concentration should be greater than 5nM but not much higher. Anything greater than 50nM is significantly overamplified.

steps.title: Submit samples for paired end sequencing
steps.instruction: Submit samples for paired end sequencing. In the NAR paper, we requested 25M reads at PE150 on NovaSeq.

steps.title: *Each transfection reaction has a max...
steps.instruction: *Each transfection reaction has a max capacity of 5 x10 6 cells and 10μL DNA. Do 20 reactions to reach 100x10 6 cells per sample. *To assess transfection efficiency, conduct an eGFP electroporation and no DNA electroporation (as unstained control). For this, only do one reaction of 5 million cells. The eGFP plasmid is ~2x the size of the ATAC-STARR library plasmids, so to match the required molarity, increase the concentration 2-fold. This control should be done for each individual cell type used in the experiment. Prepare DNA samples:

steps.title: Add 110μg ATAC-STARR library DNA into a 1
steps.instruction: Add 110μg ATAC-STARR library DNA into a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube in a max volume of 220μL [This equates to 5μg DNA per 5 million cells].

steps.title: For GFP control, add 11μg of...
steps.instruction: For GFP control, add 11μg of pcDNA3-eGFP plasmid (endotoxin-free) to a 1.5mL Eppendorf tube in a max volume of 11μL. [This equates to 10μg DNA per million cells].

steps.title: For no DNA control, add 11μL water to a 1
steps.instruction: For no DNA control, add 11μL water to a 1.5mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Prepare Suspension Cells:
steps.instruction: Prepare Suspension Cells:

steps.title: Per sample, add 50 mL of...
steps.instruction: Per sample, add 50 mL of warm recovery medium (without antibiotics) into four T-75 culture flasks. Incubate at 37°C until use.

steps.title: For each GFP or noDNA control...
steps.instruction: For each GFP or noDNA control reaction, add 10mL of media into a T-12.5 culture flasks.

steps.title: Transfer 121 million cells to as...
steps.instruction: Transfer 121 million cells to as many 50 mL falcon tube as necessary and centrifuge at 300xg for 5 minutes. Aspirate supernatant. Wash and resuspend with 45mL of DPBS or PBS, combine pellets. Centrifuge for 300xg for 5 min.

steps.title: Set up the Neon Pipette Station
steps.instruction: Set up the Neon Pipette Station: a. Fill a fresh neon tube with 3 mL of pre-warmed Buffer E2. The electrode on the side of the tube should be completely submerged in buffer. b. Insert tube into the pipette station until you hear a click. c. Replace neon tube between samples—only need to change between experimental and controls (can use the same tube for water and GFP if doing water first).

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 2178μL Buffer R/T
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 2178μL Buffer R/T. (R for GM12878, GM11831, and SUDHL-6 and T for LCL8664). Gently pipette for a single cell suspension. (Do not leave cells resuspended in Buffer R/T for longer than 15-30 min).

steps.title: Add 1980μL cells to each ATAC-STARR...
steps.instruction: Add 1980μL cells to each ATAC-STARR library DNA and gently mix.

steps.title: Add 99μL cells to GFP plasmid
steps.instruction: Add 99μL cells to GFP plasmid. Gently mix.

steps.title: Add 99μL cells to water
steps.instruction: Add 99μL cells to water. Gently mix.

steps.title: Electroporate 20 ATAC-STARR reactions and both...
steps.instruction: Electroporate 20 ATAC-STARR reactions and both control reactions (100μL at a time) using the following conditions: 100μL Neon Tip, 1100V, 30ms, 2 pulses . If the sample arcs, eject into waste and grab a new tip—19 good reactions are better than 19 good + 1 very dead. Note: Each tip can be used 5 times. Reuse the tip only if doing an aliquot of the same sample. After use, save the tip and it can be regenerated to use for another application. Regenerated tips are somewhat less efficient and more prone to arcing than fresh tips, so do not use regenerated tips in this protocol . That said, these tips are quite expensive at ~$20 each, so keep that in mind when using.

steps.title: After each electroporation, remove the pipette...
steps.instruction: After each electroporation, remove the pipette from the station and expel contents into one of the prepared T-75 (or T-12.5 for control reactions) flasks from step 6 above. Repeat electroporation with remaining aliquots and expel 5 aliquots into each T-75 flask containing 50mL media—cell concentration will be ~500,000 cells/mL. Gently swirl flask after addition of each transfected aliquot. Place in incubator at 37°C for 24 hours.

steps.title: ***We will use PI as a...
steps.instruction: ***We will use PI as a viability dye; PI will stain dead cells*** After ~24 hours, for both GFP and No DNA transfections, prepare cells for flow:

steps.title: Spin down cells in 15mL conical at 300xg, 5min
steps.instruction: Spin down cells in 15mL conical at 300xg, 5min.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 5mL 1x PBS or DPBS
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 5mL 1x PBS or DPBS.

steps.title: Spin at 300xg, 5min
steps.instruction: Spin at 300xg, 5min. Remove Supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1000μL Flow Cytometry Buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1000μL Flow Cytometry Buffer (1%BSA in 1xPBS) to achieve a cell density of ~1.25x10 6 cells/mL.

steps.title: Split each sample into two aliquots of 500μL each
steps.instruction: Split each sample into two aliquots of 500μL each. Pass through the flow cytometry tube cap filter to obtain single-cell suspensions.

steps.title: Stain one from each with propidium iodine
steps.instruction: Stain one from each with propidium iodine: Make a 1:1 dilution of 1μg/μL PI solution with 1xPBS+1%BSA. Add 1.25μL of diluted PI to the desired tubes. Vortex.

steps.title: Analyze samples with 3-laser or 5-laser Flow Cytometer
steps.instruction: Analyze samples with 3-laser or 5-laser Flow Cytometer. Use no DNA as unstained compensation. No DNA+PI and GFP noPI as compensation controls. Analyze viable GFP+PI cells for GFP/no GFP.

steps.title: Exactly 24 hours after electroporation (counting...
steps.instruction: Exactly 24 hours after electroporation (counting from last transfection added to media), mix all four T-75 flasks—200mL should all fit in one.

steps.title: Take 10 µl sample from each...
steps.instruction: Take 10 µl sample from each flask and perform cell count/assess viability.

steps.title: Divide into two 100mL volumes and process each separately
steps.instruction: Divide into two 100mL volumes and process each separately. Note: one is for RNA harvest, the other is for isolated plasmid DNA harvest. Process the RNA aliquot first as it is the most time-sensitive.

steps.title: Before processing each, spin down in...
steps.instruction: Before processing each, spin down in 50mL conical tubes, 300xg 5min. Remove sup and wash with 25mL 1x PBS or 1x DPBS. Spin down cells at 300xg, 5min and remove supernatant.

steps.title: RNA Extraction
steps.instruction: RNA Extraction: Note: Trizol and Chloroform steps should be done in a chemical fume hood.

steps.title: Add 5mL Trizol per sample (this...
steps.instruction: Add 5mL Trizol per sample (this equates to 1ml Trizol per 10e6 cells). Pipette up/down to homogenize. Note: can stop here and store at -20°C overnight.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 Invitrogen™ Phasemaker™ Tubes at 12,000xg for 30s
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 Invitrogen™ Phasemaker™ Tubes at 12,000xg for 30s.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at RT. Note
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at RT. Note: can stop here and store at -20°c overnight.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2mL chloroform to each 1mL aliquot and mix vigorously by inversion.

steps.title: Incubate for 2-3min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 2-3min at RT.

steps.title: Spin at 12,000xg for 15min @4°C
steps.instruction: Spin at 12,000xg for 15min @4°C.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous layer to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous layer to a 1.5mL Eppendorf tube—try to get ~600μL from each.

steps.title: Add 2μL Glycoblue co-precipitant (15mg/ml) to each
steps.instruction: Add 2μL Glycoblue co-precipitant (15mg/ml) to each. Do not combine like-reactions yet.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5mL isopropanol to each aqueous phase.

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 10min
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 10min.

steps.title: Spin for 10min at 12,000xg @4°C
steps.instruction: Spin for 10min at 12,000xg @4°C.

steps.title: Check for a white gel-like precipitate
steps.instruction: Check for a white gel-like precipitate. If there, remove sup and continue with protocol. If no pellet spin again.

steps.title: Resuspend Centrifuge 5 Invitrogen™ Phasemaker™ Tubesat 12,000xg for 30s
steps.instruction: Resuspend Centrifuge 5 Invitrogen™ Phasemaker™ Tubesat 12,000xg for 30s. pellet in 1mL 75% EtOH. RNA can be stored in this solution at -20°C for up to a year.

steps.title: Vortex briefly, and centrifuge 5min at 7500xg @4°C
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly, and centrifuge 5min at 7500xg @4°C. Completely discard supernatant. It helps to use a P10 after briefly spinning.

steps.title: Combine by suspending all 5 pellets in 100μL nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Combine by suspending all 5 pellets in 100μL nuclease-free water. Incubate @ 55°C for 10min.

steps.title: Determine RNA yield with Nanodrop (expected yield
steps.instruction: Determine RNA yield with Nanodrop (expected yield: 2.5-1.5μg/μL, 260/280 > 2, 260/230 > 1.8). RNA can be stored at -80°C overnight.

steps.title: Plasmid DNA extraction
steps.instruction: Plasmid DNA extraction: *Use ZymoPURE II midiprep to isolate plasmid DNA

steps.title: Follow manufactures protocol for kit
steps.instruction: Follow manufactures protocol for kit. Add 8mL P1 buffer directly to cell pellet.

steps.title: Elute in 50μL pre-warmed 10mM Tris pH 8
steps.instruction: Elute in 50μL pre-warmed 10mM Tris pH 8.0. Incubate 5min on column.

steps.title: Determine concentration and purity via nanodrop (expect ~300-400ng/μL)
steps.instruction: Determine concentration and purity via nanodrop (expect ~300-400ng/μL). Store at -20°C. These samples will be amplified in a PCR reaction in the NGS PCR step.

steps.title: *The magnetic beads are supplied at...
steps.instruction: *The magnetic beads are supplied at 5mg/mL and each mg can bind up to 5μg Poly(A)+ RNA. We will assume that Poly(A)+ RNA makes up about 5% (this is at the higher end because we are accounting for both mRNAs and the Reporter RNAs). Therefore, for 100μg Total RNA, we expect 5μg to be Poly(A)+ RNA. To capture all 5μg, we need 1mg of magnetic beads. Therefore, the ratio is 1μg Total RNA:10μg beads. Determine the amount of beads to wash:

steps.title: Wash magnetic beads:
steps.instruction: Wash magnetic beads:

steps.title: Resuspend magnetic beads in the vial (vortex for 30s)
steps.instruction: Resuspend magnetic beads in the vial (vortex for 30s).

steps.title: Transfer the desired amount to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the desired amount to a 1.5mL Eppendorf tube (use more than one tube if volume > 750μL).

steps.title: Add an equal volume of Binding Buffer and resuspend
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of Binding Buffer and resuspend.

steps.title: Place tube on DynaMag-2 magnet and incubate for 1min
steps.instruction: Place tube on DynaMag-2 magnet and incubate for 1min. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Remove tube from magnet and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from magnet and resuspend beads in the same volume of binding buffer used in step 98.3

steps.title: Add 100μL binding buffer to each 100μL Total RNA sample
steps.instruction: Add 100μL binding buffer to each 100μL Total RNA sample.

steps.title: Heat to 65°C for 2min to disrupt secondary structures
steps.instruction: Heat to 65°C for 2min to disrupt secondary structures.

steps.title: Add the indicated amount of washed...
steps.instruction: Add the indicated amount of washed beads to add to the 200μL Total RNA solution. Note: Gently handle the beads for the remaining steps of the protocol.

steps.title: Mix well and incubate on a...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate on a rotator for 5min at 4°C.

steps.title: Place tube on the magnet and incubate for ~30s
steps.instruction: Place tube on the magnet and incubate for ~30s. Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Remove tube from magnet and add...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from magnet and add the same volume used in step iv of Washing Buffer B. Mix by pipetting up/down gently.

steps.title: Incubate on magnet for ~30s and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Incubate on magnet for ~30s and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat wash for a total of 4 washes
steps.instruction: Repeat wash for a total of 4 washes.

steps.title: Add 82μL of 10mM Tris-HCl pH 7
steps.instruction: Add 82μL of 10mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5 to the beads.

steps.title: Place on magnet and quickly transfer eluate to a 1
steps.instruction: Place on magnet and quickly transfer eluate to a 1.5mL tube. Determine concentration via nanodrop (should be ~500-250ng/μL). Note: Can stop here and store at -70°C.

steps.title: *20 units are sufficient to treat...
steps.instruction: *20 units are sufficient to treat ~100μg RNA Per sample, add 10μL DNaseI Reaction buffer and 10μL DNase I (RNase-free)(20 units).

steps.title: Mix well and incubate @ 37°C for 10min
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate @ 37°C for 10min.

steps.title: Purify Poly(A)+ RNA with Zymo RNA...
steps.instruction: Purify Poly(A)+ RNA with Zymo RNA Clean & Concentrator – 25 kit. Volumes: 200μL RNA binding buffer, 300μL 100% EtOH Elute in 60μL Nuclease-Free water.

steps.title: For each 20μL reaction, the input...
steps.instruction: For each 20μL reaction, the input maximum is 1μg Poly(A)+ RNA. We expect to have ~25μg, so scale up 25-fold. Do ten 50μL reactions. Anneal primer to template RNA:

steps.title: Heat the RNA-primer mix at 65°C...
steps.instruction: Heat the RNA-primer mix at 65°C for 5min and then immediately incubate on ice for at least 1 min.

steps.title: Prepare RT reaction mix:
steps.instruction: Prepare RT reaction mix:

steps.title: Vortex and briefly centrifuge the 5x PrimeScript Buffer
steps.instruction: Vortex and briefly centrifuge the 5x PrimeScript Buffer

steps.title: Add 25μL of the MasterMix to...
steps.instruction: Add 25μL of the MasterMix to each of the 10 RNA-Primer reactions. Mix well.

steps.title: Incubate at 42°C for 60min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 42°C for 60min.

steps.title: To inactivate the reaction, heat at...
steps.instruction: To inactivate the reaction, heat at 70°C for 15min and then cool on ice.

steps.title: Pool like reactions for a total volume of ~500μL
steps.instruction: Pool like reactions for a total volume of ~500μL

steps.title: Add 1μL RNAseA (10mg/mL) and incubate 37°C for 1hr. Note
steps.instruction: Add 1μL RNAseA (10mg/mL) and incubate 37°C for 1hr. Note: final concentration is ~20μg/mL in low salt conditions [KCl] = 75mM and [MgCl 2 ] = 8mM. At low salt concentrations, RNase A cleaves single-stranded and double-stranded RNA as well the RNA strand in RNA-DNA hybrids.

steps.title: Purify cDNA using Zymo DNA Clean and Concentrate-25 kit
steps.instruction: Purify cDNA using Zymo DNA Clean and Concentrate-25 kit: a.Add 3.5mL Binding Buffer (7:1). Mix in a 15mL conical. Spin through column in 800μL increments. b.Wash with 200μL wash buffer twice. c.Elute in 28μL elution buffer.

steps.title: Nanodrop to determine yield and purity
steps.instruction: Nanodrop to determine yield and purity. Expected yield is ~10-50 ng/μL

steps.title: * To reduce rxn bias effect,...
steps.instruction: * To reduce rxn bias effect, perform two PCRs for each sample. Prepare a mastermix:

steps.title: Aliquot 39 μL mix into PCR...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 39 μL mix into PCR tubes and add 10μL of sample to each.

steps.title: Add 1μL of the correct 50μM P7 Nextera primer (N7XX)
steps.instruction: Add 1μL of the correct 50μM P7 Nextera primer (N7XX).

steps.title: Vortex and centrifuge, briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex and centrifuge, briefly. Run the following PCR reaction: 98ºC, 30 sec 8 or 12 cycles of à plasmid DNA = 8 cycles, reporter RNA = 13 cycles 98ºC, 10 sec 68ºC, 30 sec 72ºC, 30 sec Final extension 72ºC, 2 min

steps.title: Pool like-reactions and then purify cDNA...
steps.instruction: Pool like-reactions and then purify cDNA using Zymo Clean and Concentrate -5 kit – elute in 30μL elution buffer (warm buffer to 55°C or 37°C prior to elution and incubate on column for 5min).

steps.title: Determine concentration and size distribution via...
steps.instruction: Determine concentration and size distribution via HS D5000 ScreenTape on the Agilent Tapestation. Concentration should be greater than 5nM but not much higher. Anything greater than 50nM is significantly overamplified. Note: you will see the template and initial amplification products in the Tapestation, these products are not capable of generating reads on the sequencer.

steps.title: Submit samples for paired end sequencing
steps.instruction: Submit samples for paired end sequencing. In the NAR paper, we requested 50M reads for the DNA samples and 75M for the RNA samples at PE150 on NovaSeq.
